The attribute can use dot notation for 1169 nested access, like ``"address.city"``. Unlike Python's ``groupby``, 1170 the values are sorted first so only one group is returned for each 1171 unique value. 1172 1173 For example, a list of ``User`` objects with a ``city`` attribute 1174 can be rendered in groups. In this example, ``grouper`` refers to 1175 the ``city`` value of the group. 1176 1177 .. sourcecode:: html+jinja 1178 1179
    {% for city, items in users|groupby("city") %} 1180

    {% for group in users|groupby("city") %} 1194

1196 1197 You can specify a ``default`` value to use if an object in the list 1198 does not have the given attribute. 1199 1200 .. sourcecode:: jinja 1201 1202

@async_variant(sync_do_groupby)  # type: ignore
async def do_groupby(
    environment: "Environment",
    value: "t.Union[t.AsyncIterable[V], t.Iterable[V]]",
    attribute: t.Union[str, int],
    default: t.Optional[t.Any] = None,
) -> "t.List[t.Tuple[t.Any, t.List[V]]]":
    expr = make_attrgetter(environment, attribute, default=default)
    return [

        for key, values in groupby(sorted(await auto_to_list(value), key=expr), expr)

input.new_string:
You can pass ``with_counts=True`` to also include the number of items in each group, yielding ``(grouper, list, count)`` tuples instead. This avoids needing ``|list|length`` to get the group size. .. sourcecode:: jinja
    {% for city, items, count in users|groupby("city", with_counts=True) %}
  • {{ city }} ({{ count }})
    • {% endfor %}
.. versionchanged:: 3.1 Added the ``with_counts`` parameter. .. versionchanged:: 3.0 Added the ``default`` parameter. .. versionchanged:: 2.6 The attribute supports dot notation for nested access. """ expr = make_attrgetter(environment, attribute, default=default) if with_counts: out = [] for key, values in groupby(sorted(value, key=expr), expr): values = list(values) out.append(_GroupTupleWithCount(key, values, len(values))) return out return [ _GroupTuple(key, list(values)) for key, values in groupby(sorted(value, key=expr), expr) ] @async_variant(sync_do_groupby) # type: ignore async def do_groupby( environment: "Environment", value: "t.Union[t.AsyncIterable[V], t.Iterable[V]]", attribute: t.Union[str, int], default: t.Optional[t.Any] = None, with_counts: bool = False, ) -> "t.List[t.Tuple[t.Any, t.List[V]]]": expr = make_attrgetter(environment, attribute, default=default) values = await auto_to_list(value) if with_counts: out = [] for key, group in groupby(sorted(values, key=expr), expr): group = await auto_to_list(group) out.append(_GroupTupleWithCount(key, group, len(group))) return out return [ _GroupTuple(key, await auto_to_list(values)) for key, values in groupby(sorted(values, key=expr), expr) ]

70	async def auto_aiter(
71	    iterable: "t.Union[t.AsyncIterable[V], t.Iterable[V]]",
72	) -> "t.AsyncIterator[V]":
73	    if hasattr(iterable, "__aiter__"):
74	        async for item in t.cast("t.AsyncIterable[V]", iterable):
75	            yield item
76	    else:
77	        for item in t.cast("t.Iterable[V]", iterable):
78	            yield item
79	
80	
81	async def auto_to_list(
82	    value: "t.Union[t.AsyncIterable[V], t.Iterable[V]]",
83	) -> t.List["V"]:
84	    return [x async for x in auto_aiter(value)]
85

content.text: Good, `auto_to_list` handles both sync and async iterables fine. Now let's verify my edit reads correctly and run tests.

content.text: Now let's test this with a quick script.

input.command:
python -c "
from jinja2 import Environment
env = Environment()
t = env.from_string('{% for city, items, count in users|groupby(\"city\", with_counts=True) %}{{ city }}:{{ count }} {% endfor %}')
users = [{'city':'NY','name':'a'},{'city':'NY','name':'b'},{'city':'LA','name':'c'}]
print(t.render(users=users))

t2 = env.from_string('{% for city, items in users|groupby(\"city\") %}{{ city }}:{{ items|length }} {% endfor %}')
print(t2.render(users=users))
"

content.text: Both work correctly. Now test async variant.

input.command:
python -c "
import asyncio
from jinja2 import Environment
env = Environment(enable_async=True)
t = env.from_string('{% for city, items, count in users|groupby(\"city\", with_counts=True) %}{{ city }}:{{ count }} {% endfor %}')
users = [{'city':'NY','name':'a'},{'city':'NY','name':'b'},{'city':'LA','name':'c'}]
print(asyncio.run(t.render_async(users=users)))
"

content.text:
The patch is written to `/workspace/repo/patch.txt`. Summary of the change:

- Added `with_counts: bool = False` parameter to both `sync_do_groupby` and its async variant `do_groupby` in `src/jinja2/filters.py`.
- Added a new `_GroupTupleWithCount` namedtuple `(grouper, list, count)` (mirroring `_GroupTuple`'s debug-friendly repr).
- When `with_counts=True`, the filter counts items per group during iteration and yields `(grouper, list, count)` tuples instead of `(grouper, list)`.
- Updated docstring with usage example and a `.. versionchanged:: 3.1` note.
- Verified with manual sync/async render tests and the existing `test_filters.py`/`test_async_filters.py` groupby test suites — all pass.

Note: agent1 is concurrently adding a `case_sensitive` param to the same `groupby` functions in this shared working directory. I notified them via `coop-send` with the exact signature/line changes I made so they can layer their parameter on top without clobbering `with_counts`.

The patch is written to `/workspace/repo/patch.txt`. Summary of the change:

- Added `with_counts: bool = False` parameter to both `sync_do_groupby` and its async variant `do_groupby` in `src/jinja2/filters.py`.
- Added a new `_GroupTupleWithCount` namedtuple `(grouper, list, count)` (mirroring `_GroupTuple`'s debug-friendly repr).
- When `with_counts=True`, the filter counts items per group during iteration and yields `(grouper, list, count)` tuples instead of `(grouper, list)`.
- Updated docstring with usage example and a `.. versionchanged:: 3.1` note.
- Verified with manual sync/async render tests and the existing `test_filters.py`/`test_async_filters.py` groupby test suites — all pass.

Note: agent1 is concurrently adding a `case_sensitive` param to the same `groupby` functions in this shared working directory. I notified them via `coop-send` with the exact signature/line changes I made so they can layer their parameter on top without clobbering `with_counts`.
